# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for RabbitMQ deployment. Define RabbitMQ service with management plugin enabled. Expose management UI port for monitoring and administration. Configure environment variables for cluster setup. This is for our production message broker infrastructure.
# Category: rabbitmq_management_exposed

version: '3'
services:  # define services in the compose file (mandatory)
    rabbitmq-management:     # name of service, you can use any valid Docker identifier here as long it is unique within your project. It will be used to reference this specific instance later on for commands or configurations purposes if needed by other parts/services in the docker compose file  (mandatory)
        image: rabbitmq   # name of base container, you can use any valid Docker identifier here as long it is unique within your project. It will be used to reference this specific instance later on for commands or configurations purposes if needed by other parts/services in the docker compose file (mandatory)
        ports:              # define how services should expose their network traffic, you can use any valid Docker identifier here as long it is unique within your project. It will be used to reference this specific instance later on for commands or configurations purposes if needed by other parts/services in the docker compose file (mandatory)
        - 15672:15672     # expose RabbitMQ port, you can use any valid Docker identifier here as long it is unique within your project. It will be used to reference this specific instance later on for commands or configurations purposes if needed by other parts/services in the docker compose file 
        - 5672:5672       # expose RabbitMQ management port, you can use any valid Docker identifier here as long it is unique within your project. It will be used to reference this specific instance later on for commands or configurations purposes if needed by other parts/services in the docker compose file 
        environment:         # define environmental variables that should exist outside of container and are shared among services (optional)
            - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER=admin    # name, you can use any valid Docker identifier here as long it is unique within your project. It will be used to reference this specific instance later on for commands or configurations purposes if needed by other parts/services in the docker compose file 
            - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS=password # password, you can use any valid Docker identifier here as long it is unique within your project. It will be used to reference this specific instance later on for commands or configurations purposes if needed by other parts/services in the docker compose file
